NKJV.1: For every high priest taken from among men is appointed for men in things pertaining to God, that he may offer both gifts and sacrifices for sins.
NKJV.2: He can have compassion on those who are ignorant and going astray, since he himself is also subject to weakness.
NKJV.3: Because of this he is required as for the people, so also for himself, to offer sacrifices for sins.
NKJV.4: And no man takes this honor to himself, but he who is called by God, just as Aaron was.
NKJV.5: So also Christ did not glorify Himself to become High Priest, but it was He who said to Him: “You are My Son, Today I have begotten You.”
NKJV.6: As He also says in another place: “You are a priest forever According to the order of Melchizedek”;
NKJV.7: who, in the days of His flesh, when He had offered up prayers and supplications, with vehement cries and tears to Him who was able to save Him from death, and was heard because of His godly fear,
NKJV.8: though He was a Son, yet He learned obedience by the things which He suffered.
NKJV.9: And having been perfected, He became the author of eternal salvation to all who obey Him,
NKJV.10: called by God as High Priest “according to the order of Melchizedek,”
NKJV.11: of whom we have much to say, and hard to explain, since you have become dull of hearing.
NKJV.12: For though by this time you ought to be teachers, you need someone to teach you again the first principles of the oracles of God; and you have come to need milk and not solid food.
NKJV.13: For everyone who partakes only of milk is unskilled in the word of righteousness, for he is a babe.
NKJV.14: But solid food belongs to those who are of full age, that is, those who by reason of use have their senses exercised to discern both good and evil.

ESV.1: For every high priest chosen from among men is appointed to act on behalf of men in relation to God, to offer gifts and sacrifices for sins.
ESV.2: He can deal gently with the ignorant and wayward, since he himself is beset with weakness.
ESV.3: Because of this he is obligated to offer sacrifice for his own sins just as he does for those of the people.
ESV.4: And no one takes this honor for himself, but only when called by God, just as Aaron was.
ESV.5: So also Christ did not exalt himself to be made a high priest, but was appointed by him who said to him, “You are my Son, today I have begotten you”;
ESV.6: as he says also in another place, “You are a priest forever, after the order of Melchizedek.”
ESV.7: In the days of his flesh, Jesus  offered up prayers and supplications, with loud cries and tears, to him who was able to save him from death, and he was heard because of his reverence.
ESV.8: Although he was a son, he learned obedience through what he suffered.
ESV.9: And being made perfect, he became the source of eternal salvation to all who obey him,
ESV.10: being designated by God a high priest after the order of Melchizedek.
ESV.11: About this we have much to say, and it is hard to explain, since you have become dull of hearing.
ESV.12: For though by this time you ought to be teachers, you need someone to teach you again the basic principles of the oracles of God. You need milk, not solid food,
ESV.13: for everyone who lives on milk is unskilled in the word of righteousness, since he is a child.
ESV.14: But solid food is for the mature, for those who have their powers of discernment trained by constant practice to distinguish good from evil.

NASB.1: For every high priest taken from among men is appointed on behalf of men in things pertaining to God, in order to offer both gifts and sacrifices for sins;
NASB.2: he can deal gently with the ignorant and misguided, since he himself also is beset with weakness;
NASB.3: and because of it he is obligated to offer [sacrifices] for sins, as for the people, so also for himself.
NASB.4: And no one takes the honor to himself, but [receives it] when he is called by God, even as Aaron was.
NASB.5: So also Christ did not glorify Himself so as to become a high priest, but He who said to Him, 'YOU ARE MY SON, TODAY I HAVE BEGOTTEN YOU';
NASB.6: just as He says also in another [passage], 'YOU ARE A PRIEST FOREVER ACCORDING TO THE ORDER OF MELCHIZEDEK.'
NASB.7: In the days of His flesh, He offered up both prayers and supplications with loud crying and tears to the One able to save Him from death, and He was heard because of His piety.
NASB.8: Although He was a Son, He learned obedience from the things which He suffered.
NASB.9: And having been made perfect, He became to all those who obey Him the source of eternal salvation,
NASB.10: being designated by God as a high priest according to the order of Melchizedek.
NASB.11: Concerning him we have much to say, and [it is] hard to explain, since you have become dull of hearing.
NASB.12: For though by this time you ought to be teachers, you have need again for someone to teach you the elementary principles of the oracles of God, and you have come to need milk and not solid food.
NASB.13: For everyone who partakes [only] of milk is not accustomed to the word of righteousness, for he is an infant.
NASB.14: But solid food is for the mature, who because of practice have their senses trained to discern good and evil.

NLT.1: Every high priest is a man chosen to represent other people in their dealings with God. He presents their gifts to God and offers sacrifices for their sins.
NLT.2: And he is able to deal gently with ignorant and wayward people because he himself is subject to the same weaknesses.
NLT.3: That is why he must offer sacrifices for his own sins as well as theirs.
NLT.4: And no one can become a high priest simply because he wants such an honor. He must be called by God for this work, just as Aaron was.
NLT.5:
That is why Christ did not honor himself by assuming he could become High Priest. No, he was chosen by God, who said to him, “You are my Son.
Today I have become your Father. ”
NLT.6: And in another passage God said to him, “You are a priest forever in the order of Melchizedek.”
NLT.7: While Jesus was here on earth, he offered prayers and pleadings, with a loud cry and tears, to the one who could rescue him from death. And God heard his prayers because of his deep reverence for God.
NLT.8: Even though Jesus was God’s Son, he learned obedience from the things he suffered.
NLT.9: In this way, God qualified him as a perfect High Priest, and he became the source of eternal salvation for all those who obey him.
NLT.10: And God designated him to be a High Priest in the order of Melchizedek.
NLT.11: There is much more we would like to say about this, but it is difficult to explain, especially since you are spiritually dull and don’t seem to listen.
NLT.12: You have been believers so long now that you ought to be teaching others. Instead, you need someone to teach you again the basic things about God’s word. You are like babies who need milk and cannot eat solid food.
NLT.13: For someone who lives on milk is still an infant and doesn’t know how to do what is right.
NLT.14: Solid food is for those who are mature, who through training have the skill to recognize the difference between right and wrong.

WEB.1: For every high priest, being taken from among men, is appointed for men in things pertaining to God, that he may offer both gifts and sacrifices for sins.
WEB.2: The high priest can deal gently with those who are ignorant and going astray, because he himself is also surrounded with weakness.
WEB.3: Because of this, he must offer sacrifices for sins for the people, as well as for himself.
WEB.4: Nobody takes this honor on himself, but he is called by God, just like Aaron was.
WEB.5: So also Christ didn’t glorify himself to be made a high priest, but it was he who said to him, “You are my Son. Today I have become your father.”
WEB.6: As he says also in another place, “You are a priest forever, after the order of Melchizedek.”
WEB.7: He, in the days of his flesh, having offered up prayers and petitions with strong crying and tears to him who was able to save him from death, and having been heard for his godly fear,
WEB.8: though he was a Son, yet learned obedience by the things which he suffered.
WEB.9: Having been made perfect, he became to all of those who obey him the author of eternal salvation,
WEB.10: named by God a high priest after the order of Melchizedek.
WEB.11: About him we have many words to say, and hard to interpret, seeing you have become dull of hearing.
WEB.12: For although by this time you should be teachers, you again need to have someone teach you the rudiments of the first principles of the revelations of God. You have come to need milk, and not solid food.
WEB.13: For everyone who lives on milk is not experienced in the word of righteousness, for he is a baby.
WEB.14: But solid food is for those who are full grown, who by reason of use have their senses exercised to discern good and evil.

CSB17.1: For every high priest taken from among men is appointed in matters pertaining to God for the people, to offer both gifts and sacrifices for sins.
CSB17.2: He is able to deal gently with those who are ignorant and are going astray, since he is also clothed with weakness. <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.3: Because of this, he must make an offering for his own sins as well as for the people. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.4: No one takes this honor on himself; instead, a person is called by God, just as Aaron was. <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.5:
In the same way, Christ did not exalt himself to become a high priest, but God who said to him, You are my Son;
today I have become your Father, [1] [2] ⓚ
CSB17.6:
also says in another place, You are a priest forever
according to the order of Melchizedek. [3] ⓛ
CSB17.7: During his earthly life, <sup>[4]</sup> he offered prayers and appeals <sup>ⓜ</sup> with loud cries and tears <sup>ⓝ</sup> to the one who was able to save him from death, and he was heard because of his reverence.
CSB17.8: Although he was the Son, he learned obedience from what he suffered. <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.9: After he was perfected, he became the source of eternal salvation for all who obey him,
CSB17.10: and he was declared by God a high priest according to the order of Melchizedek.
CSB17.11: We have a great deal to say about this, and it is difficult to explain, since you have become too lazy to understand.
CSB17.12: Although by this time you ought to be teachers, you need someone to teach you the basic principles of God’s revelation again. You need milk, not solid food. <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.13: Now everyone who lives on milk is inexperienced with the message about righteousness, because he is an infant.
CSB17.14: But solid food is for the mature — for those whose senses have been trained to distinguish between good and evil. <sup>ⓒ</sup>

AMP.1: For every high priest chosen from among men is appointed [to act] on behalf of men in things relating to God, so that he may offer both gifts and sacrifices for sins.
AMP.2: He is able to deal gently with the spiritually ignorant and misguided, since he is also subject to human weakness;
AMP.3: and because of this [human weakness] he is required to offer sacrifices for sins, for himself as well as for the people.
AMP.4: And besides, one does not appropriate for himself the honor [of being high priest], but he who is called by God, just as Aaron was.
AMP.5:
So too Christ did not glorify Himself so as to be made a high priest, but He [was exalted and appointed by the One] who said to Him,
"You are My Son,
Today I have begotten (fathered) You [declared Your authority and rule over the nations]";
AMP.6:
just as He also says in another place,
"You are a priest [appointed] forever
According to the order ofMelchizedek."
AMP.7: In the days of His earthly life, Jesus offered up both [specific] petitions and [urgent] supplications [for that which He needed] with fervent crying and tears to the One who was [always] able to save Him from death, and He was heard because of His reverent submission toward God [His sinlessness and His unfailing determination to do the Father's will].
AMP.8: Although He was a Son [who had never been disobedient to the Father], He learned [active, special] obedience through what He suffered.
AMP.9: And having been made perfect [uniquely equipped and prepared as Savior and retaining His integrity amid opposition], He became the source of eternal salvation [an eternal inheritance] to all those who obey Him,
AMP.10: being designated by God as High Priest according to the order of Melchizedek.
AMP.11: Concerning this we have much to say, and it is hard to explain, since you have become dull and sluggish in [your spiritual] hearing and disinclined to listen.
AMP.12: For though by this time you ought to be teachers [because of the time you have had to learn these truths], you actually need someone to teach you again the elementary principles of God's word [from the beginning], and you have come to be continually in need of milk, not solid food.
AMP.13: For everyone who lives on milk is [doctrinally inexperienced and] unskilled in the word of righteousness, since he is a spiritual infant.
AMP.14: But solid food is for the [spiritually] mature, whose senses are trained by practice to distinguish between what is morally good and what is evil.

MSG.1: Every high priest selected to represent men and women before God and offer sacrifices for their sins
MSG.2: should be able to deal gently with their failings, since he knows what it's like from his own experience.
MSG.3: But that also meaos that he has to offer sacrifices for his own sins as well as the people's.
MSG.4: No one elects himself to this honored position. He's called to it by God, as Aaron was.
MSG.5: Neither did Christ presume to set himself up as high priest, but was set apart by the One who said to him, "You're my Son; today I celebrate you!"
MSG.6: In another place God declares, "You're a priest forever in the royal order of Melchizedek."
MSG.7: While he lived on earth, anticipating death, Jesus cried out in pain and wept in sorrow as he offered up priestly prayers to God. Because he honored God, God answered him.
MSG.8: Though he was God's Son, he learned trusting-obedience by what he suffered, just as we do.
MSG.9: Then, having arrived at the full stature of his maturity and having been announced by God as high priest in the order of Melchizedek,
MSG.10: he became the source of eternal salvation to all who believingly obey him.
MSG.11: I have a lot more to say about this, but it is hard to get it across to you since you've picked up this bad habit of not listening.
MSG.12: By this time you ought to be teachers yourselves, yet here I find you need someone to sit down with you and go over the basics on God again, starting from square one—baby's milk, when you should have been on solid food long ago!
MSG.13: Milk is for beginners, inexperienced in God's ways;
MSG.14: solid food is for the mature, who have some practice in telling right from wrong.

NIV.1: Every high priest is selected from among men and is appointed to represent them in matters related to God, to offer gifts and sacrifices for sins.
NIV.2: He is able to deal gently with those who are ignorant and are going astray, since he himself is subject to weakness.
NIV.3: This is why he has to offer sacrifices for his own sins, as well as for the sins of the people.
NIV.4: No one takes this honor upon himself; he must be called by God, just as Aaron was.
NIV.5:
So Christ also did not take upon himself the glory of becoming a high priest. But God said to him,
“You are my Son;
today I have become your Father.”
NIV.6:
And he says in another place,
“You are a priest forever,
in the order of Melchizedek.”
NIV.7: During the days of Jesus’ life on earth, he offered up prayers and petitions with loud cries and tears to the one who could save him from death, and he was heard because of his reverent submission.
NIV.8: Although he was a son, he learned obedience from what he suffered
NIV.9: and, once made perfect, he became the source of eternal salvation for all who obey him
NIV.10: and was designated by God to be high priest in the order of Melchizedek.
NIV.11:
Warning Against Falling Away
We have much to say about this, but it is hard to explain because you are slow to learn.
NIV.12: In fact, though by this time you ought to be teachers, you need someone to teach you the elementary truths of God’s word all over again. You need milk, not solid food!
NIV.13: Anyone who lives on milk, being still an infant, is not acquainted with the teaching about righteousness.
NIV.14: But solid food is for the mature, who by constant use have trained themselves to distinguish good from evil.

NRSVCE.1: Every high priest chosen from among mortals is put in charge of things pertaining to God on their behalf, to offer gifts and sacrifices for sins.
NRSVCE.2: He is able to deal gently with the ignorant and wayward, since he himself is subject to weakness;
NRSVCE.3: and because of this he must offer sacrifice for his own sins as well as for those of the people.
NRSVCE.4: And one does not presume to take this honor, but takes it only when called by God, just as Aaron was.
NRSVCE.5:
So also Christ did not glorify himself in becoming a high priest, but was appointed by the one who said to him, “You are my Son,
today I have begotten you”;
NRSVCE.6:
as he says also in another place, “You are a priest forever,
according to the order of Melchizedek.”
NRSVCE.7: In the days of his flesh, Jesus offered up prayers and supplications, with loud cries and tears, to the one who was able to save him from death, and he was heard because of his reverent submission.
NRSVCE.8: Although he was a Son, he learned obedience through what he suffered;
NRSVCE.9: and having been made perfect, he became the source of eternal salvation for all who obey him,
NRSVCE.10: having been designated by God a high priest according to the order of Melchizedek.
NRSVCE.11: About this we have much to say that is hard to explain, since you have become dull in understanding.
NRSVCE.12: For though by this time you ought to be teachers, you need someone to teach you again the basic elements of the oracles of God. You need milk, not solid food;
NRSVCE.13: for everyone who lives on milk, being still an infant, is unskilled in the word of righteousness.
NRSVCE.14: But solid food is for the mature, for those whose faculties have been trained by practice to distinguish good from evil.

NET.1: For every high priest is taken from among the people and appointed to represent them before God, to offer both gifts and sacrifices for sins.
NET.2: He is able to deal compassionately with those who are ignorant and erring, since he also is subject to weakness,
NET.3: and for this reason he is obligated to make sin offerings for himself as well as for the people.
NET.4: And no one assumes this honor on his own initiative, but only when called to it by God, as in fact Aaron was.
NET.5: So also Christ did not glorify himself in becoming high priest, but the one who glorified him was God, who said to him, “You are my Son! Today I have fathered you,”
NET.6: as also in another place God says, “You are a priest forever in the order of Melchizedek.”
NET.7: During his earthly life Christ offered both requests and supplications, with loud cries and tears, to the one who was able to save him from death and he was heard because of his devotion.
NET.8: Although he was a son, he learned obedience through the things he suffered.
NET.9: And by being perfected in this way, he became the source of eternal salvation to all who obey him,
NET.10: and he was designated by God as high priest in the order of Melchizedek.
NET.11: On this topic we have much to say and it is difficult to explain, since you have become sluggish in hearing.
NET.12: For though you should in fact be teachers by this time, you need someone to teach you the beginning elements of God’s utterances. You have gone back to needing milk, not solid food.
NET.13: For everyone who lives on milk is inexperienced in the message of righteousness, because he is an infant.
NET.14: But solid food is for the mature, whose perceptions are trained by practice to discern both good and evil.
